name: Harbinger
classification_notes: Described as geometric and collegiate with low contrast and high personality, placing it firmly in display territory designed for impact rather than extended reading.
summary: A thick, low-contrast geometric display face built for layouts that need visual weight and presence.
description: Harbinger is a chunky, geometric display typeface with a collegiate character and low stroke contrast. It is designed for headlines and layouts where directness and visual punch are the priority. The foundry's own copy positions it as a workhorse for design that needs extra force.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Harbinger as thick, cut and sturdy, a geometric collegiate typeface with low contrast and high personality suited to layouts that need extra punch and power.

name: Dirt Nap
classification_notes: An all-caps decorative display face with western and medieval influences and ornate detailing, placing it firmly in the display category with an ornate subcategory.
summary: An all-caps display face drawing on western and medieval references, with ornate detailing suited to logo and identity work.
description: Dirt Nap blends western, medieval and vaguely magical visual cues into an all-caps display typeface with ornate detailing. The foundry positions it as highly customisable and well-suited to logo and identity projects where personality and distinctiveness are priorities. Its decorative character makes it a strong choice for short-form settings rather than extended text.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Dirt Nap as a little western, a little medieval and a little magical: an all-cap display type with ornate detailing that adds personality and flair to any design. They highlight its customisability and recommend it for logo and identity projects.

name: Yarmouth
classification_notes: A display block font with deliberate imperfections evoking handmade signage; sits closest to a hand-lettered display category given its intentional irregularity and vernacular roots.
summary: A deliberately imperfect block display font drawn from the tradition of handmade coastal signage in New England.
description: Yarmouth leans into the irregularity of hand-painted lettering, giving it a warmth that polished display fonts rarely achieve. The block construction references vernacular sign-painting traditions of coastal New England, making it well-suited to branding, packaging and editorial work that calls for an authentic, handmade character. At $25 with a commercial licence add-on available, it is an accessible option for regional or artisanal projects.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Yarmouth as a personably imperfect block font that evokes handmade signs and the feel of coastal New England.

name: Stiff Arm
classification_notes: A monoline display face with angular, wave-like letterforms that mimic curves through sharp edges rather than true rounds, placing it firmly in the display category.
summary: A monoline display face that replaces conventional curves with sharp, wavy edges to create an energetic, offbeat character.
description: The letterforms use angular, wave-like constructions in place of true curves, giving the face an irregular, hand-drawn quality that sits somewhere between geometric and organic. It suits headlines and short-form display work where a distinctive, unconventional silhouette is needed. The single weight and monoline stroke keep things simple whilst the wavy forms carry the visual interest.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Stiff Arm as a monoline display face with sharp edges that simulate letterform curves, noting that the wavy forms add personality and an unexpected charm to typography.

name: Revenant
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a condensed display serif with expressive, imperfect character terminals and uneven lining, placing it firmly in the display category rather than a workhorse serif tradition.
summary: A condensed display serif with deliberately uneven lining and clumsy terminals that give it an unapologetic, handmade quality.
description: Revenant is built around intentional imperfection: its condensed forms carry uneven baselines and irregular terminals that give words set in it a specific, slightly raw voice. The design sits squarely in display territory, suited to editorial headlines or branding where a polished grotesque or clean serif would read as too composed. Available under a commercial licence add-on, it is priced accessibly for independent use.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Revenant as expressive, human and imperfect, a condensed display serif whose characters carry an unapologetic quality. Uneven lining and clumsy terminals are treated as features rather than flaws, lending a distinct voice and tone to any words set in the face.

name: Raided Premises
classification_notes: Hand-rendered lettering based on a specific historical signage source, placing it firmly in the display category as a decorative, contextually loaded typeface rather than a text face.
summary: A two-weight hand-rendered display face drawn from the police notice posted at The Stonewall Inn following the 1969 raid.
description: The letterforms are taken directly from a piece of historical signage: the notice police posted at The Stonewall Inn in 1969. Released in two weights, the family carries an explicit political and cultural context that gives it a specificity most display faces lack. Best suited to editorial, activist or commemorative work where the historical reference adds meaning rather than noise.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Raided Premises as a hand-rendered type offered in two weights, inspired by the sign posted at The Stonewall Inn by police after the 1969 raid. It is made in homage to those who fought and continue to fight for equality, and is framed as a snapshot of history tied to the origins of the Queer rights movement.

name: Avalon
classification_notes: A chunky, bold, rounded typeface intended for display and headline use; the rounded terminals and heavy weight place it firmly in the rounded display category.
summary: A chunky rounded display face suited to headlines and short bursts of text rather than sustained reading.
description: Heavy proportions and rounded terminals give Avalon a blunt, friendly character that works in poster headlines and subheadings. The foundry advises against body copy use, positioning it as a personality piece for short-form layout work. A single weight is offered with a complete glyph set.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Avalon as a simple, bold, rounded typeface well suited to display, headlines and subheads, noting that while its chunky nature limits use in body copy, short sentences can add personality to any layout or design. It comes with a complete glyph set.

name: Doughboy
classification_notes: A chunky, rounded display face with a soft, personality-driven aesthetic; fits the display category with no apparent text-weight applications.
summary: A chunky, rounded display face built for bold logotypes, loud headlines and typographic executions with a soft, characterful presence.
description: Doughboy is a soft, voluminous display face designed to fill space with visual weight and personality. The rounded forms suit logotype work, poster headlines and bold typographic statements where warmth and bulk are the priority. Glyph coverage extends into Latin Extended for broader language support.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Doughboy as a chunky and soft display face that takes up space nicely and carries a lot of personality, suited to bold typographic executions, logo types and loud statements, with a glyph set that includes extended Latin.

name: Something Sans
classification_notes: Described as rooted in the geo-grotesque aesthetic with blocky, simplified forms; sits at the intersection of geometric and grotesque sans traditions.
summary: A four-style geo-grotesque with blocky forms and a rounded variant that adds a softer edge to its simplified shapes.
description: Built on a geo-grotesque base, Something Sans pairs blocky construction with occasional unexpected letterform choices that give it personality without sacrificing legibility. The family runs to four styles across two sub-families: the standard cut in Light and Regular, and a Rounded version in the same weights. The rounded variant softens the blunt terminals to lend the family a friendlier register.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Something Sans as a type family with roots in the geo-grotesque aesthetic, with blocky forms and simplistic, sometimes unexpected shapes that give it charm while maintaining clarity. It includes Light, Regular, Rounded Light and Rounded styles.

name: Eventide
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a display typeface drawing on Art Deco forms and sign painting traditions, placing it firmly in the display category with an art deco subcategory.
summary: A two-weight display face that merges Art Deco geometry with the hand-painted quality of sign painting traditions.
description: Drawing on the geometric rigour of Art Deco alongside the craft sensibility of sign painting, Eventide is offered in Light and Regular weights as a bundled pair. The combination of historical reference points gives it a period character suited to editorial headlines, branding and packaging where a decorative yet structured letterform is needed.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe YRT Eventide as a display typeface that draws inspiration from the Art Deco movement and synthesises it with sign painting aesthetics, sold as a bundle containing both the Regular and Light weights.

name: Dropout
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a blocky collegiate sans-serif with stencil uppercase variants and an intentionally imperfect character, placing it firmly in display rather than text use.
summary: A blocky collegiate sans with built-in stencil uppercase glyphs and a deliberately rough, imperfect personality.
description: Built for display use, Dropout leans on the chunky proportions of collegiate lettering with an added stencil variant baked into the uppercase. The imperfect quality is intentional, giving it warmth and character rather than the sterile precision of a cleaned-up digital font. Diacritics, numerals and punctuation are included, making it reasonably complete for short-form display work.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Dropout as a blocky collegiate sans-serif with a charmingly imperfect personality. Uppercase glyphs include a stencil version, and the font ships with a full set of diacritical marks, numerals, punctuation and additional glyphs.

name: Ruckus
classification_notes: A purely decorative, abstract display typeface with three stylistic variants; its limited glyph set and expressive, squishy letterforms place it firmly in the display category with no useful subcategory beyond that.
summary: An abstract, uppercase-only display face in three stylistic variants — cutout, filled and outline — built for high-impact, short-text applications.
description: Ruckus pairs a squishy, expressive quality with three distinct styles that allow layering or standalone use across posters and logo work. Its glyph set is deliberately limited to uppercase letters and basic punctuation, keeping it focused on impact over versatility. At £25 for a single family it sits as an affordable option for designers needing a loose, energetic headline face.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Ruckus as an abstract, squishy, playful and loud display type offered in three styles: cutout, filled and outline. They recommend it for impactful typography in applications requiring few words, such as posters and logo designs, noting its glyph set is limited to uppercase and a handful of punctuation marks.

name: Gnasher
classification_notes: Super blocky, hand-rendered block letterforms designed for display use; echoes informal marker and notebook lettering traditions rather than any formal type classification.
summary: A chunky block-letter display face with alternating letterforms that mimic the irregular quality of hand-drawn notebook lettering.
description: Built around oversized, heavily blocky forms, Gnasher mimics the kind of marker lettering that fills school notebooks. Both upper and lowercase include letterform variations to break up mechanical repetition and preserve a hand-rendered feel. Suited to streetwear graphics, zines and any project needing blunt, casual weight.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Gnasher as a super blocky font reminiscent of the block letters found on high school notebooks, with upper and lowercase variations across each letter to introduce a hand-rendered quality into type layouts.

name: Adult Skate
classification_notes: Described as falling between hand-lettering and cursive with curvilinear forms and a playful personality, placing it firmly in the casual script category.
summary: A mono-weight casual script sitting between hand-lettering and cursive, suited to short phrases and single words.
description: Curvilinear letterforms give this script a loose, playful character without veering into full calligraphic territory. The glyph set covers upper and lowercase, numerals and special Latin characters, with alternate terminals on lowercase glyphs for a more individual look. Works best at display sizes in small runs of text rather than extended setting.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Adult Skate as a script font falling somewhere between hand-lettering and cursive, with a playful personality expressed through mono-weight curvilinear forms. They recommend it for short phrases and individual words, and note alternate terminals on lowercase glyphs for a more custom appearance.

name: Alpiner
classification_notes: A structured display typeface with hand-rendered qualities, sharp corners and irregular widths place it firmly in the display category, drawing from vintage alpine poster lettering traditions.
summary: A display typeface referencing old Swiss ski poster lettering, with sharp corners and deliberate irregularities giving it a hand-drawn character.
description: Drawn from the visual language of vintage Swiss alpine posters, this structured display face uses sharp corners, varying stroke widths and small idiosyncratic details to suggest hand lettering within a disciplined framework. The result sits between geometric rigidity and craft imperfection, making it well suited to poster work, sporting brands and retro-inflected editorial contexts.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Alpiner as inspired by old Swiss ski posters, with sharp corners, irregular widths and small details used to bring a hand-rendered quality to an otherwise structured display font.

name: War Machine
classification_notes: An all-caps grotesque inspired by turn-of-the-century signage lettering, placing it squarely in the display grotesque tradition with vernacular roots.
summary: An all-caps display grotesque in four weights, drawn from the vernacular lettering of turn-of-the-century signage.
description: Inspired by hand-painted and printed signage from around 1900, War Machine offers four weights from Light to Bold in an all-caps grotesque format. The historical reference gives it a period warmth that sets it apart from clean, constructed grotesques. Suited to editorial headlines, branding and poster work where a vernacular edge is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe War Machine as inspired by old turn-of-the-century signage, offering an all-caps grotesque type family across four weights: Light, Regular, Medium and Bold.

name: Knuckler
classification_notes: An all-caps hand-rendered display face with stencil alternates and decorative catchwords; clearly intended for expressive, informal display use rather than text setting.
summary: An all-caps hand-lettered display face evoking school notebook doodles, with a stencil alternate style and decorative catchwords, arrows and manicules.
description: Knuckler draws on the rough, imperfect quality of hand-drawn notebook lettering, kept deliberately casual in its stroke weight and construction. The capitals double as an alt stencil cut, adding a second visual register without a separate font file. Script catchwords, arrows and manicules are included to support hand-crafted editorial and packaging layouts.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Knuckler as reminiscent of school-day notebook lettering, blending an imperfect, hand-rendered quality with a playful spirit. It is an all-caps font with an alt stencil style contained in the capitals, and includes script catchwords, arrows and manicules alongside the standard glyph set.

name: Aphelion
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Aphelion as a humanist sans, with contrast between thick and thin strokes characteristic of that subtype.
summary: A humanist sans with measured stroke contrast, designed for flexible use across a range of tones and applications.
description: Aphelion draws on humanist sans conventions, balancing thicker and thinner parts of each character to produce a face that reads as both warm and adaptable. The contrast gives it more character than a neutral geometric, without committing to the quirkiness of a fully calligraphic humanist. Suited to editorial, branding and interface work where a human quality is wanted without overt personality.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Aphelion as striking a balance between the thicker and thinner parts of each character, with its humanist-sans qualities making it a versatile choice for a range of tones and personalities.

name: Hijinx
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a compressed, sturdy sans serif display type, placing it firmly in the display category with a compressed sans subcategory.
summary: A compressed, sturdy sans serif display face offered in regular and italic with full upper and lowercase coverage and expanded Latin punctuation.
description: Built for headlines and display settings, Hijinx pairs a compressed structure with a solid, weighty presence. Both regular and italic styles are included, with upper and lowercase letterforms and an expanded Latin character set covering punctuation and diacritics. The compressed proportions make it well suited to tight poster layouts and editorial headers.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Hijinx as a compressed and sturdy sans serif display type, available in regular and italic styles with upper and lowercase letterforms and expanded Latin punctuation and characters.

name: Omicron
classification_notes: Described as an irregular, chunky display face with a hand-rendered style, placing it firmly in the display category with a vernacular, handmade subcategory.
summary: A chunky, irregular display face that borrows from hand-rendered lettering while retaining enough structure to stay legible.
description: Omicron sits in the space between casual hand lettering and structured type design, with irregular forms and chunky strokes giving it a raw, drawn quality. It retains sufficient legibility to function in display settings where personality is the priority. Well-suited to branding, posters and editorial contexts that call for an imperfect, handmade aesthetic.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Omicron as an irregular, chunky display face that offers a hand-rendered style with enough structure and legibility to bring a distinct character to designs.

name: Iron Ranger
classification_notes: Rectangular letterform construction with rounded corners and uniform character widths places this firmly in geometric display territory, inspired by National Parks signage vernacular.
summary: A geometric display face drawn from National Parks signage, with rectangular letterforms softened by rounded corners.
description: Rectangular construction gives each character a consistent footprint, lending the family an even texture that works across varied layout contexts. Rounded corners prevent the rigidity you might expect from such a strict grid, carrying just enough warmth to avoid feeling cold or industrial. The National Parks reference is specific and coherent, anchoring the personality without overstating it.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Iron Ranger as inspired by National Parks signage, combining simple sophistication with personality through rounded corners. Character forms are constructed within a rectangular framework to ensure uniformity across the set, which they say allows for greater flexibility in design applications.

name: Afterburner
classification_notes: Described explicitly as an extended, heavy-weight display face drawing from grotesque traditions; the extended width and display-only positioning push it into the display category rather than a general-purpose grotesque.
summary: A single-weight extended display face drawn from grotesque traditions, built for heavy, large-scale typesetting.
description: Afterburner is a wide, heavy display face that revisits the proportions and character of historical grotesque typefaces through a contemporary lens. Available as a single weight, it is designed for headlines and poster work where bold extended letterforms command attention. The licensing model separates a base purchase from commercial add-ons, suggesting it is aimed at studio and brand use.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Afterburner as an extended, heavy-weight display face that takes cues from grotesque typefaces of the past with a modern eye.

name: Lucky Dog
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a display type with blocky, curvy forms and medium contrast; the rounded, playful character shapes place it firmly in the display sans category rather than a functional text grotesque.
summary: A blocky, rounded display sans with medium contrast and a curvy, approachable character, sold as a single style.
description: Lucky Dog is a display sans built around blocky forms softened by curved details, giving it a bold presence that reads as playful rather than aggressive. Medium contrast runs through the letterforms, adding a little structure without tipping into formality. Both upper and lowercase are included, broadening its use across headlines, logos and packaging where a friendly, confident voice is needed.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Lucky Dog as a blocky, curvy sans serif display type with both upper and lowercase. They note that its medium-contrast characters and curvy, playful shapes give it a bold but accessible personality.

name: Spacemagic
classification_notes: An all-caps display typeface with an explicitly retro, funky aesthetic inspired by mid-century space-age imagery, placing it firmly in the display category with a retro subcategory.
summary: An all-caps display face with a retro space-age character, drawn from childhood memories of planetarium visits.
description: Spacemagic is an all-capitals display typeface from Yeahright Type with a funky, retro personality rooted in mid-century space-age aesthetics. The design takes its cues from the wonder and unease of school planetarium trips, giving it a nostalgic quality suited to headlines, posters and branding where personality is the priority. Sold as a single style with a commercial licence add-on available.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Spacemagic as a funky all-cap display with old-school retro vibes, inspired by school field trips to the planetarium and the sense of magic and fear that space conjured then, and still does today.

name: Ginza Loop
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif with rounded characteristics, placing it squarely in the geometric sans subcategory with soft, rounded terminals.
summary: A rounded geometric sans built for high-visibility applications such as wayfinding, packaging and out-of-home work.
description: Soft, rounded terminals give this geometric sans a friendly legibility without tipping into novelty. The foundry positions it for practical, functional contexts where clarity at scale matters, including signage, packaging and advertising. Its simplicity is intentional rather than underdeveloped.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Ginza Loop as a modern geometric sans serif with rounded characteristics and a simplistic personality that avoids feeling bland, suited to wayfinding signage, out-of-home, packaging and other projects requiring higher visibility.

name: Lodestar
classification_notes: Described as neo-grotesque with a monospace-esque quality and simple geometric structure, placing it within the grotesque sans tradition but with a contemporary geometric influence.
summary: A neo-grotesque sans with monospace-esque proportions, built for legibility at small sizes and impact at display scale.
description: Lodestar sits between past and present with a simple geometric structure that reads at body sizes without losing its presence at headline scale. The single style covers uppercase, lowercase, numerals and an expanded punctuation set. Its monospace-influenced spacing gives it a fixed, methodical rhythm unusual for a grotesque.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Lodestar as a neo-grotesque, monospace-esque typeface with one foot in the past and one in the present. Its simple geometric structure supports legibility at small sizes while remaining impactful at headline sizes, and the set includes all lowercase and uppercase, numerals and an expanded range of punctuation and glyphs.

name: Bodega Cat
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a hand written, mono-line typeface; the casual, informal letterforms and cursive alternate capitals place it firmly in the casual handwritten script subcategory.
summary: A mono-line handwritten typeface with oversized capitals and cursive alternate glyphs for informal display use.
description: Drawn as a single-weight, mono-line script, it pairs slim regular letterforms with oversized capitals that give the family its distinctive rhythm. Cursive alternate glyphs for the capitals allow designers to vary the feel within a single setting. Best suited to packaging, branding and display work where an approachable, hand-drawn quality is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Bodega Cat as a hand written, mono-line typeface featuring slim and oversized capitals. Alternate glyphs for the capital letters introduce a cursive element into any design they appear in.

name: Copperhead
classification_notes: Thick, loud lettering with varied line weight and intentional imperfections; designed explicitly for display use with a raw, expressive character.
summary: A thick, raw display typeface with varied stroke weight and deliberate imperfections aimed at loud, expressive design work.
description: Built with intentional roughness, Copperhead uses varied line weight and surface irregularity to give it a handmade, assertive character. It is pitched squarely at bold, distinctive display applications where polish would undercut the effect. Offered as a single style at a low entry price with a commercial licence add-on available.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Copperhead as thick, loud and raw, with varied line weight and intentional imperfections that set it apart as a display face ready for bold and distinct design projects.

name: Murphy
classification_notes: Described as a hybrid display/text serif with a thin, restrained character, suggesting a refined display serif that holds up at smaller sizes.
summary: A thin, restrained display serif with an eccentric origin story, covering standard Latin glyphs and functioning across both large and small sizes.
description: Murphy is a hybrid display and text serif with a thin, controlled character that sits comfortably at headline scale while remaining legible at smaller settings. The design draws its personality from an unlikely muse, described as a chunky dog with an odd tail, lending it a subtle idiosyncrasy beneath its restrained exterior. Character set covers standard Latin and Latin-1 Supplement glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Murphy as a thin, restrained serif inspired by a chunky, ferocious dog with an odd tail. They position it as a hybrid display and text typeface that works well at large sizes and effectively at smaller type sizes, with a character set covering all standard Latin and Latin-1 Supplement glyphs.

name: Cheap Thrills
classification_notes: Condensed and hand-drawn in character, Cheap Thrills sits firmly in the display category as a hand-rendered sans with a rough, gritty aesthetic suited to logos and badges rather than text setting.
summary: A condensed, hand-drawn sans with a rough, gritty character aimed at badge and logo work that calls for a human touch.
description: Drawn with an intentionally imperfect hand, Cheap Thrills leans into the kind of worn, tactile quality that suits patch badges, logotypes and brand marks with a street or craft edge. The character set covers the full alphabet, numerals and an extended punctuation set alongside bonus glyphs. Its tight condensed proportions and gritty texture make it readable at display sizes while retaining plenty of personality.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Cheap Thrills as a condensed, hand-rendered sans typeface with a fistful of grit, containing a full alphabet, numerals, extended punctuation and bonus glyphs. They position it as a typeface that shines in badges and logos calling for a human-touch aesthetic.

name: Culture Vulture
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a rounded display typeface, drawing on multiple historical type eras and styles for an eclectic decorative character.
summary: A rounded display face drawing on multiple historical type eras, sold as a single style with full character, numeral and punctuation coverage.
description: Culture Vulture pulls from a range of type history and stylistic periods to arrive at a rounded, expressive display character. It ships with a full character set, numerals and punctuation, making it ready for immediate use in titles, posters and branding. The single-style offering keeps it focused and straightforward for projects needing a nostalgic, eclectic touch.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Culture Vulture as a rounded display typeface inspired by multiple eras and styles of type, equipped with a full character and numeral set along with a range of punctuation.

name: Moon Valley
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a thick, hand-lettered serif display type with vintage and woodblock character; the ornamental, expressive quality places it firmly in the display category.
summary: A thick, hand-lettered serif display face with a vintage woodblock character, sold as a single style.
description: Drawn with hand-lettered warmth and a woodblock weight, this display serif carries the kind of rough, deliberate quality associated with antique print ephemera. Named after a specific place meaningful to the studio, it reads as a personal project rather than a commissioned commission. At a single style and a low price point, it suits headlines, labels and short-form display work where a period feel is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Moon Valley as named after a special place, presenting it as a thick, hand-lettered serif display typeface with a vintage and woodblock feel to its character set.

name: Wide Rule
classification_notes: Hand-rendered letterforms imitating classroom note-taking and doodles place this firmly in the casual handwritten script category, with no formal calligraphic structure.
summary: A casual hand-rendered typeface evoking the scrawled notes and doodles of a school notebook, offered in regular and italic styles.
description: Drawn to mimic the informal lettering of classroom note-taking and margin doodles, Wide Rule carries the uneven rhythm and personality of genuine handwriting. The family comes in two styles, regular and italic, and the foundry suggests it works particularly well at small sizes for support copy and short passages. Latin diacritical characters have since been added, broadening its language support.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Wide Rule as a hand-rendered type reminiscent of school notes and doodles, designed to pass the time in class. Available in regular and italic, it is recommended at small sizes for support copy, small words or short sentences, with an update adding latin diacritical characters.

name: Lady Victory
classification_notes: Wide, squared letterforms with hand-lettered personality place this firmly in the display category; the squared geometry and eclectic character distinguish it from conventional grotesques or geometric sans families.
summary: A wide, squared display typeface with hand-lettered character, suited to headlines, strap lines and catch words.
description: Wide letterforms and squared geometry give Lady Victory a bold, block-like presence at large sizes. Hand-lettered eccentricities add warmth and prevent the squareness from feeling mechanical. Best used for display headlines and short lines where its personality can read clearly.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Lady Victory as a wide, squared display typeface with eclectic, hand-lettered personality, suited to large display sizes as well as smaller strap lines and catch words.

name: Bloodsucka
classification_notes: An irregular condensed display sans with two layerable styles; the unconventional letterforms and layering system place it firmly in the display category rather than functional text use.
summary: A condensed irregular display sans in two layerable styles designed for headline use with a raw, hand-touched character.
description: Two styles, Filled and Drained, work independently or in combination, with Drained sitting atop Filled to produce an outlined effect. The irregular letterforms give it a hand-made, slightly unsettled quality suited to music, editorial or horror-adjacent branding. At $25 it is accessible, though its single weight and narrow application scope limit its versatility.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Bloodsucka as an irregular condensed display sans in two styles. The Filled style works alone or as a base layer, with the Drained style placed on top to add an outline around the characters.

name: Nice Weather
classification_notes: Bold, condensed and rounded, with a hand-rendered quality that places it firmly in the display category. The rounded terminals and condensed proportions make it unsuitable for text use.
summary: A bold condensed display face with fully rounded edges and a hand-rendered character, priced accessibly at $25.
description: Rounded terminals and condensed proportions give this face a loose, approachable quality that sits apart from more rigid display types. The hand-rendered feeling adds personality without tipping into illegibility, making it suited to branding, posters and packaging where a warmer voice is needed. A straightforward single-weight offering at an entry-level price point.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Nice Weather as a bold and condensed display face with all the edges rounded off, designed to inject a hand-rendered feeling and extra character into designs.

name: Tough Luck
classification_notes: The name and foundry context suggest a display typeface; no further classification detail is available as the page returned a 503 error.
summary: No page content was available at the time of retrieval due to a server error.
description: The page returned a 503 Service Unavailable error, so no details about weights, styles, designer, year or language support could be confirmed. All fields have been set to conservative defaults pending a successful page load.
foundry_blurb: No foundry copy was available as the page was unreachable at time of retrieval.

name: Replicant
classification_notes: A narrow, mono-width sans serif with geometric characteristics, offered in regular and rounded styles, intended for all-caps display use.
summary: A narrow mono-width sans in two styles, regular and rounded, built for all-caps setting across scales from giant headlines to body copy.
description: Replicant is a mono-width, narrow sans serif designed to work in all-caps configurations, from large single-word headlines down to small body text. Two styles, regular and rounded, give it a degree of flexibility within a fairly specific formal register. Its even stroke width and tight proportions make it suited to display work where consistent texture and density matter.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Replicant as a versatile, narrow, mono-width sans serif available in regular and rounded styles, noting it works well set in all-caps as a single oversized word, a headline, or even small body copy.

name: Mama Earth
classification_notes: Thick, bulbous letterforms with wavy organic contours place this firmly in the display category, drawing on the psychedelic and hand-lettered aesthetic of 1960s and early 1970s graphic design.
summary: A thick, wavy display typeface with bulbous forms drawn from the organic lettering styles of the 1960s and early 1970s.
description: Mama Earth takes its cues from the hand-crafted, psychedelic type aesthetics of the 1960s and early 70s, with swelling, irregular contours and chunky strokes that give it a distinctly period character. The wavy letterforms and rounded details make it well suited to branding or editorial projects that call for a retro or countercultural flavour. Available as a single style at a modest price point, with a commercial licence add-on available separately.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Mama Earth as a thick, organic display typeface taking cues from type design of the 60s and early 70s, with wavy forms and bulbous characteristics that infuse a quirky quality into designs.

name: Bang Bang!
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a thick, bold, grotesque-influenced display face with deliberately personalised and livened letterforms that deviate from strict grotesque conventions.
summary: A thick, bold display face that takes a grotesque foundation and pushes the letterforms toward something more characterful and expressive.
description: Built on a grotesque skeleton but deliberately departing from it, the typeface trades neutrality for personality in its letter construction. The weight sits firmly in the heavy end, making it suited to headlines, posters and branding where a grotesque feel is wanted without the restraint of a conventional one. Available for commercial use with an add-on licence.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Bang Bang! as a thick and bold grotesque-esque typeface with a grotesque foundation that deviates to infuse more personality into the forms, likening it to a family friend who feels familiar but isn't quite what they appear.

name: False Alarm
classification_notes: A condensed, collegiate-style display face with bold, compressed letterforms typical of sports and varsity typography; the 'Dulled' variant adds rounded corners for a stamp-like quality.
summary: A condensed collegiate display face with two styles, including a softened 'Dulled' variant with rounded corners and a stamp-like quality.
description: Built for maximum impact in a tight column, this condensed display face draws on collegiate and varsity typographic traditions. The standard style carries a punchy, assertive character, while the Dulled variant introduces rounded corners and a pseudo-stamp texture that dials back the aggression. At $25 for personal use it sits well within reach for small projects and self-initiated work.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe False Alarm as a condensed collegiate-style display face with plenty of character for the space it occupies. Two styles are offered: Regular and Dulled, the latter featuring rounded corners and a pseudo-stamp feel intended to soften the overall impression.

name: Souvenirs
classification_notes: Marketed explicitly as a display typeface with extended and semi-condensed glyph variants, suggesting decorative intent over text use.
summary: A display typeface offering switchable extended and semi-condensed glyphs so designers can mix width variants within a single setting.
description: Each character is available in both extended and semi-condensed forms, allowing custom combinations that vary rhythm and density across a word or headline. The approach gives a single-weight purchase a degree of expressive range usually reserved for multi-width families. Suited to editorial headlines, poster work and branding where optical contrast within a word is the point.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Souvenirs as a contemporary display typeface that lets you choose between extended or semi-condensed glyphs to create your own unique combinations, with the name inspired by the French word for memories.

name: Fat Cat
classification_notes: Extreme thick-thin contrast and display-only sizing place this firmly in the high-contrast display category, with a decorative serif character suited to headlines and large-scale setting.
summary: A high-contrast display serif with exaggerated thicks and thins, designed for large-scale setting with an air of old-money elegance.
description: Fat Cat works at headline and poster sizes where its dramatic thick-thin contrast can register properly. The foundry points to the numerals as a particular strength, suggesting a character suited to editorial and luxury contexts. Best used in mixed case or all lowercase at generous sizes.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Fat Cat as a jumbo typeface full of thicks and thins for a sophisticated look, recommending it at large sizes in mixed case or all lowercase, and calling out the numerals as particularly expressive of an old-money sensibility.

name: Post No Bills
classification_notes: A block stencil typeface with deliberate urban roughness; the stencil construction and imperfect letterforms place it firmly in display territory.
summary: A block stencil face with deliberate roughness and imperfection, drawn from urban signage and street typography.
description: Built on simple, blocky letterforms, Post No Bills introduces grit and irregularity that stop it reading as a clean geometric stencil. The imperfections are intentional, giving the face the kind of wear associated with hand-cut stencils or city street markings. Best suited to display use where a raw, urban character is called for.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Post No Bills as a block stencil typeface inspired by the sights and sounds of city life, with a little urban grit and imperfection giving it humanity alongside the simple letterforms at its base.

name: School No. 9
classification_notes: A slim display typeface with retro styling drawn from historical school signage; the narrow proportions and vintage aesthetic place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A slim retro display face inspired by a historic Indianapolis schoolhouse associated with the Vonnegut family.
description: Drawn from the signage and lettering tradition of the old Clemens Vonnegut school in Indianapolis, School No. 9 is a narrow display typeface with a vintage American flavour. The slim proportions give it a distinctly period feel while remaining legible at headline sizes. Suited to retro-leaning branding, editorial headings and ephemera where a touch of Midwestern nostalgia is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe School No. 9 as a slim typeface with retro charm and a modern feeling, inspired by the historic Clemens Vonnegut school in Indianapolis, connected to the family of the celebrated American author Kurt Vonnegut.

name: Izanami
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans with monospace influence, placing it squarely in the geometric sans subcategory with a functional, grid-based character.
summary: A geometric sans with deliberate monospace influence, designed for versatility across visual styles.
description: Built on simple geometric forms with a nod to monospaced construction, Izanami aims for broad applicability without committing to a single aesthetic register. The extended Latin glyph set makes it usable across a range of Western languages. Its restraint is both its strength and its limitation, offering neutrality rather than a strong typographic voice.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Izanami as a simple geometric sans with monospace flavour, noting that its simplicity makes it extremely versatile across multiple visual aesthetics. The glyph set includes extended Latin.

name: Rosamond
classification_notes: An all-caps ultra-condensed modern display face intended purely for large headlines, placing it firmly in the display category with a condensed modern subcategory.
summary: An all-caps ultra-condensed modern display face built for large-scale headline use.
description: A single-weight, all-caps ultra-condensed typeface designed for maximum impact at headline sizes. The character set covers capitals, numerals and punctuation, keeping the scope tight and the intent clear. Best suited to posters, title cards and editorial headers where vertical compression and strong vertical stress do the heavy lifting.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Rosamond as an all-caps ultra-condensed modern display typeface suited to large headlines and words for maximum impact, including a full set of all-cap characters, numerals and punctuation.

name: Shishito Sans
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a humanist sans by the foundry; all-caps construction and playful character suggest a display-oriented humanist sans designed for large-scale use.
summary: An all-caps humanist sans with a playful disposition, built for packaging, menu design and logo work at display sizes.
description: A single-weight, all-caps humanist sans designed to perform best when set large. The glyph set covers numerals and a full punctuation complement, giving it practical range despite its display nature. The foundry positions it for packaging, menu and logo design where a slightly irreverent character suits the brief.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Shishito Sans as a playful, slightly spicy humanist sans offered in all caps and numerals, with an expansive glyph set and full punctuation. They note it is flexible at smaller sizes but shines when set large, recommending it for packaging, menu and logo design.

name: Apricot Sans
classification_notes: A wide, ultra-heavy display face with expressive proportions; its extended width and extreme weight place it firmly in the display category, designed for large-scale use rather than text setting.
summary: An extended, ultra-heavy display sans with a playful, organic character suited to large-scale headline work.
description: A wide and thick display sans with a warmth that suits food, lifestyle and market-facing brands. The family covers uppercase letters, numerals and punctuation only, keeping its scope tight and its personality legible at a glance. Best deployed at large sizes where its generous proportions and expressive weight read as intended.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Apricot Sans as an extended width, extra thick and juicy display face with all the character of the fruit stand at the farmers market, including uppercase letters, numerals and punctuation.

name: Butch Queen
classification_notes: An engraved-style display typeface with both filled and outlined variants, suited to badge, packaging and lockup work rather than extended text setting.
summary: A two-style engraved display face balancing feminine and masculine qualities, suited to badge work, packaging and typographic lockups.
description: Comes in filled and outlined cuts, giving designers a quick pairing for layered or two-colour treatments. The engraved aesthetic lends itself to heritage-adjacent badge design and packaging, while its dual-gendered character keeps it from reading as purely period or nostalgic. A single-weight family priced for independent and small-studio use.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Butch Queen as an engraved-style typeface that balances feminine and masculine qualities in one face. It includes two styles, filled and outlined, and is recommended for badge designs, packaging and typographic lockups.

name: Canoe Rental
classification_notes: A hand-lettered all-caps display typeface with an informal, casual character; the deliberate irregularity and off-kilter variant place it firmly in the hand-rendered display category.
summary: A hand-lettered display typeface offered in aligned and off-kilter variants, evoking the casual warmth of hand-painted signage.
description: Built around all-caps letterforms with lowercase alternates, the family comes in two subfamilies: one neatly aligned, one deliberately askew for a looser, more spontaneous feel. Coverage extends to numerals and basic punctuation. It works at small sizes as well as display scale, making it adaptable for branding, packaging and editorial contexts that call for a human, unhurried quality.
foundry_blurb: Yeahright Type describe Canoe Rental as a hand-rendered typeface reminiscent of time spent on the water, flexible enough for both small and display type. It is offered in aligned and off-kilter versions and includes all caps with lowercase alternates, numerals and some punctuation, lending a familiar and human touch to designs.
